page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 62 GB of RAM, translating to 7.75 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture, it contains 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation. The processor operates at a base speed of 2.3 GHz and features a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.96 GB/sec, Geekbench single-core execution at 2270.0, and Geekbench text processing at 3059.0, all placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Single-threaded gzip compression is strong at 34.83 GB/sec, placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 7129.54 and Geekbench multi-core scoring 8519.0, both falling in the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth is measured at 687.78 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 61.4 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance exhibits weak performance in larger workloads, such as text generation on a medium 7B LLM and prompt processing on a large 70B LLM.

The c4-highmem-8 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration, avoiding the added cost of those components. However, the lack of local storage means all data persistence relies on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. The instance is suited for memory-intensive tasks such as database operations, Redis caching, text processing, and software compilation. It is less suitable for large-scale LLM text generation or heavy multi-threaded parallel computing due to its average multi-core scaling and weak large-model inference metrics.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized instance configured with 8 vCPUs and 62 GB of RAM, running on a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 4 physical cores, 2 threads per core, and a 260 MB L3 cache, but lacks local storage and GPU resources. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the top 10% for single-core Geekbench scores and single-threaded bzip3 decompression, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth fall within the average range. It exhibits weak performance for medium-to-large LLM inference tasks. Qualitatively, the instance provides a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads that do not require local storage overhead. It is suited for database operations, Redis caching, text processing, and software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 server with 8 vCPUs, 62 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It delivers top-tier single-core performance but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 server with 8 vCPUs and 62 GB RAM, featuring top-tier single-core performance and no local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server with dedicated Intel Xeon processing, designed for database operations, caching, and single-threaded efficiency.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 62 GB RAM powered by a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and single-threaded decompression benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, reducing overhead for network-reliant workloads
Equipped with Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instruction sets
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and software compilation
